title: California Senate Moves Forward with SB348 to Revise LCFS
content: On Wednesday, the California State Senate Environmental Quality Committee unanimously approved the Low Carbon Fuel Standard Revision Act (CA S.B. 348), sending the bill to the State Assembly Committee on Appropriations. Introduced by Senator Melissa Hurtado, the legislation mandates the California Air Resources Board (CARB) to review and revise the state's Low Carbon Fuel Standard (LCFS) program by January 31, 2026, with the aim of lessening the financial impact on California drivers. The bill requires CARB to conduct a thorough review of the costs of carbon credits passed onto consumers, focus on reforms that support drivers, and incorporate economic equity into all future statewide climate initiatives.
author: Analysts Account
